title: rosa eglanteria var. bishopii
intro: Rosa eglanteria var. bishopii, also known as Rose of Sharon or Althea, is a flowering plant with showy, pink to purple flowers that is native to China and Japan. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, with the roots and flowers being used to make herbal remedies. The health benefits of Rosa eglanteria var. bishopii include its ability to reduce fever, soothe sore throats, and promote wound healing, making it a popular ingredient in teas, tinctures, and salves. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying the roots and flowers to make a powder or tea that can be used to treat various health conditions.
